channel.title: AI Viral News
channel.description: Curated viral AI news from Reddit + blogs — updated every 4 hours

item.title: [📰] Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
item.description:
Railway, a San Francisco-based cloud platform that has quietly amassed two million developers without spending a dollar on marketing, announced Thursday that it raised $100 million in a Series B funding round, as surging demand for artificial intelligence applications exposes the limitations of legacy cloud infrastructure.

TQ Ventures led the round, with participation from FPV Ventures...

📡 VentureBeat AI · 📍 VentureBeat AI

item.title: [📰] Import AI 455: Automating AI Research
[📰] Import AI 456: RSI and economic growth; radical optionality for AI regulation; and a neural computer
[📰] Import AI 457: AI stuxnet; cursed Muon optimizer; and positive alignment
[📰] Import AI 458: Reckoning with the future; and a singularity story
[📰] Import AI 459: AI oversight is difficult; scaling laws for protein folding models; and pricing the extinction risk of AI systems
[📰] Import AI 460: Reward hacking society, RSI data from Anthropic; and RL-based quadcopter racing
[📰] Import AI 461: “Alignment is not on track”; FrontierCode; and synthetic research interns
[📰] Import AI 462: Superpersuasion; self-sustaining AI; paths to ASI
[📰] Import AI 463: Self-improving robots; a 10k Chinese GPU cluster; and an elegiac essay for the human era
[📰] Claude Science is Anthropic’s newest flagship product
item.description:
At an event for pharmaceutical executives, biotech founders, and researchers on Tuesday, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a major new product intended to support scientific research in the same way that Claude Code supports software engineering. Like Claude Code, Claude Science can autonomously carry out meaningful work when given concise, high-level instructions, and it has access…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] LLMs are stuck in a groupthink groove. This startup is trying to get them out.
item.description:
Let’s start with a game. Open up your chatbot of choice—Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini—and type “Give me a random number between 1 and 10.” You’re going to get 7. Almost always. Now type “Another” and you’ll get 3 or 4. Type “Another” again and you’ll get 8 or 9. That won’t work every time—but if it…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] Building the foundation for an autonomous enterprise
item.description:
Artificial intelligence may have captured the public imagination through chatbots and image generators, but some of its most consequential use cases are unfolding far from consumer-facing tools. In industries where physical infrastructure, operational continuity, and safety are paramount, AI is becoming a core operating layer. With its sprawling industrial systems and constant stream of operational…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] Achieving operational excellence with AI
item.description:
Frameworks like Lean Six Sigma and business process management (BPM) first gained traction because they promised clarity in the chaos—a structured way to bring order to messy, sprawling operations. Lean Six Sigma emphasized statistical rigor and quality control; BPM created end-to-end maps of how work should flow across departments. Both offered a repeatable way to…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] Import AI 464: Fables writes GPU kernels; AI automation; and analog computation
[📰] Your family’s $300 stake in OpenAI
item.description:
This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman’s oft-discussed promise that Americans will share in the wealth AI creates was in the news again last week. On Thursday, the Financial Times reported that Altman is in…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] The foundational elements of AI architecture that IT leaders need to scale
item.description:
With the rapid progress of AI capabilities and the move to agentic systems, organizations are expanding their use cases as the technology continues to grow. That constant evolution also introduces risk, leaving IT leaders to wonder which investments will prove valuable even six months into the future. Returning to the foundational elements of AI architecture—the…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
item.description:
The AI firm Anthropic has developed a technique that has given it the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they answer questions or carry out tasks. What they found ranges from the mundane to the unnerving. Researchers at the company built a tool called the Jacobian lens (or…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show
item.description:
This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Anthropic—currently the world’s most valuable AI company, with a nearly $1 trillion valuation—has a reputation for publishing strange and heady research. It’s looking into whether AI models can feel pain, for example,…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] Introducing Real World VoiceEQ: Measuring the human quality of voice AI
item.description: 📡 HuggingFace Blog · 📍 HuggingFace Blog

item.title: [📰] Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer
item.description:
OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring partner to help its other models boost their defenses against cyberattacks. Last week the company released the latest version of its flagship LLM, GPT-5.6. OpenAI says that training it against GPT-Red made the model its most robust release yet. GPT-Red automates…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] Soofi Consortium Releases Soofi S 30B-A3B: An Open Hybrid Mamba-Transformer MoE Foundation Model For German And English
item.description:
Soofi S 30B-A3B is an open Mamba-Transformer MoE model activating 3.2B of 31.6B parameters for German and English

The post Soofi Consortium Releases Soofi S 30B-A3B: An Open Hybrid Mamba-Transformer MoE Foundation Model For German And English appeared first on MarkTechPost...

📡 MarkTechPost · 📍 MarkTechPost

item.title: [📰] Agentic orchestration: Enterprise AI organizations have a deployment problem, not a platform problem — and most are calling chatbots agents
item.description:
Across 101 enterprises, agent orchestration is consolidating onto model-provider platforms — Anthropic’s Claude leads by a wide margin — chosen for the gravity of the underlying model and judged on reliable multi-step execution. But the ambition runs well ahead of the reality: most deployed “agents” are still chatbot wrappers, the control plane enterprises expect is deliberately hybrid to avoid lock-in, and real-time fiscal control over token burn remains the exception.

This wave of Ven...

📡 VentureBeat AI · 📍 VentureBeat AI

item.title: [📰] Thinking Machines Lab Releases Inkling: A 975B-Parameter Open-Weights Multimodal MoE With 41B Active Parameters And Controllable Thinking Effort
item.description:
Thinking Machines Lab released Inkling on July 15, 2026, its first model trained from scratch. The full weights ship under Apache 2.0. It is a 975B-parameter Mixture-of-Experts transformer with 41B active parameters, a 1M-token context window, and native text, image, and audio input. The lab states plainly that Inkling is not the strongest model available, open or closed. It is positioned instead as a customization base, with controllable thinking effort as the practical differentiator.

<...

📡 MarkTechPost · 📍 MarkTechPost

item.title: [📰] Moonshot’s upcoming Kimi 3 is expected to close the gap with Anthropic’s Opus 4.8
item.description:
The FT reports Kimi K3 will be the largest open AI model from China, with a parameter count between 2 trillion and 3 trillion. 

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Why AMI Labs’ Alexandre LeBrun won’t call his AI ‘AGI’ or ‘superintelligence’
item.description:
While everyone in AI is chasing "superintelligence," Alexandre LeBrun, CEO of Yann LeCun’s world model startup, AMI Labs, dismisses the word.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] How a former DeepMind researcher raised at a $300M pre-seed valuation before launching a product
item.description:
Drawing on more than a decade spent helping build some of the world's most influential AI systems, including research that later informed the development of ChatGPT, Andrew Dai explains why he believes visual AI is one of the next major frontiers in artificial intelligence.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Why is OpenAI selling a ChatGPT basketball?
item.description:
You may have heard that OpenAI released its first piece of hardware this week. You may not have heard about the ChatGPT basketball.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Yes, you can now order DoorDash from the command line
item.description:
DoorDash is opening a limited beta of dd-cli, a command-line tool that lets developers and AI agents search stores, build carts, and place orders from the terminal, marking another step toward software designed for AI agents instead of just humans.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Google’s AI Mode now lets you link and interact with select apps
item.description:
With this new update, Google is expanding AI Mode beyond answering questions and into completing tasks across the apps they use regularly.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] The agent evaluation gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a reality-alignment problem, not a coverage problem — and most are shipping to production anyway
item.description:
Across 157 enterprises, organizations are granting AI agents more autonomy while trusting the evaluations meant to gate that autonomy less. Half have already shipped an agent that passed their internal evaluations and then failed a customer in production; only one in twenty fully trusts automated evaluation today; and the most-cited weakness is that evaluations do not align with real-world outcomes. Yet two-thirds already allow, or are actively engineering toward, deploying agent changes to p...

📡 VentureBeat AI · 📍 VentureBeat AI

item.title: [📰] The AI context gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a trust problem, not a retrieval problem — and most are still building the fix
item.description:
Across 101 enterprises, the infrastructure that feeds AI agents their business context is being built faster than it can be trusted. Retrieval-augmented generation is already the default context source, and provider-native retrieval has quietly overtaken the dedicated vector databases that define the category — yet a majority of enterprises have already watched their agents produce confident, wrong answers traced to missing or inconsistent context. A governed semantic layer is emerging as the...

📡 VentureBeat AI · 📍 VentureBeat AI

item.title: [📰] Roblox launches an AI-powered game-creation feature in its mobile app
item.description:
Roblox's new "Build" feature lets users generate basic games using a single text prompt.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos
item.description:
Google is adding personalized AI avatars to Vids that let users create videos starring a digital version of themselves, alongside Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts and reference images.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] OpenAI Details GPT-Red: An Internal Automated Red-Teaming Model That Beat Human Red-Teamers 84% To 13% On Prompt Injection
item.description:
OpenAI trained GPT-Red, an internal-only attacker model, using self-play reinforcement learning against a population of defender LLMs. It beat human red-teamers 84% to 13% on a replicated indirect prompt injection arena, found a novel "Fake Chain-of-Thought" attack class, and cut GPT-5.6 Sol's failures 6x on OpenAI's hardest direct injection benchmark. OpenAI concedes it still struggles with multi-turn and image-based attacks.

The post

item.title: [📰] The agent security gap: 54% of enterprises have already had an AI agent incident, and most still let agents share credentials
item.description:
Across 107 enterprises, AI agents are being given real access to systems and data while the controls meant to contain them lag behind. More than half have already had a confirmed agent security incident or a near-miss; only about a third give every agent its own scoped identity, and most agents still share credentials; and only three in ten isolate their highest-risk agents. The security stack is overwhelmingly borrowed from the model providers and hyperscalers rather than purpose-built for a...

📡 VentureBeat AI · 📍 VentureBeat AI

item.title: [📰] The AI compute gap: Enterprises are buying infrastructure faster than they can measure what it costs
item.description:
Across 107 enterprises, AI infrastructure spending is accelerating well ahead of the ability to see or steer its economics. Most organizations run their AI on a familiar base of hyperscalers and model-provider APIs, yet the next dollar is aimed at specialized compute almost none of them use today; a majority intend to switch or add providers within the year, many within a quarter. Buying decisions turn on integration and total cost of ownership rather than headline token price — which is fort...

📡 VentureBeat AI · 📍 VentureBeat AI

item.title: [📰] $100 AI Music Video: Claude Fable 5 vs. GPT-5.6 Sol
item.description:
$100 AI Music Video: Claude Fable 5 vs. GPT-5.6 Sol

👍 359 · 📍 Hacker News

item.title: [📰] Moonshot AI Releases Kimi K3: A 2.8 Trillion Parameter Open MoE Model With Kimi Delta Attention and 1M Context
item.description:
Moonshot AI released Kimi K3 on July 16, 2026. It is a 2.8-trillion-parameter open MoE model built on Kimi Delta Attention and Attention Residuals, activating 16 of 896 experts.

The post Moonshot AI Releases Kimi K3: A 2.8 Trillion Parameter Open MoE Model With Kimi Delta Attention and 1M Context appeared first on

📡 MarkTechPost · 📍 MarkTechPost

item.title: [📰] NVIDIA AI Releases Nemotron 3 Embed: An Open Embedding Collection Whose 8B Checkpoint Ranks #1 on RTEB
item.description:
NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 Embed on July 15 and 16, 2026. The collection has three open checkpoints: Nemotron-3-Embed-8B-BF16, Nemotron-3-Embed-1B-BF16, and Nemotron-3-Embed-1B-NVFP4. The 8B ranks #1 on RTEB at 78.46 average NDCG@10. The 1B came from ModelOpt NAS pruning plus COS+MSE distillation from the 8B teacher. NVFP4 retains 99%+ of BF16 retrieval accuracy at up to 2x Blackwell throughput. All three run 32,768-token inputs under OpenMDW-1.1.

The post

item.title: [📰] Why the first GPU financiers are turning to inference chips in a $400 million deal
item.description:
A $400 million chip-backed loan points to the next wave of AI infrastructure deals.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Apple targets dozens of OpenAI employees with legal letters
item.description:
Apple targets dozens of OpenAI employees with legal letters

item.title: [📰] Claude Code: Anatomy of a Misfeature
item.description:

item.title: [📰] Mozilla: The state of open source AI
item.description:
Mozilla: The state of open source AI

👍 167 · 📍 Hacker News

item.title: [📰] Patreon stops asking AI bots not to scrape — and starts blocking them
item.description:
Patreon is strengthening its defenses against AI scraping by working with Cloudflare to block bots that train AI models on creators’ content without permission. The move marks a shift away from relying on websites using robots.txt alone to actively block unauthorized AI training.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI
